prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `c87289bb`.
codex_response:
Implemented `c87289bb` end to end. The rule is: treat the blue `8` cells in the top row as vertical streams, extend each stream to the bottom, and if a stream would pass through a one-row red `2` blocker, reroute it across the row above the blocker to the nearest open side column, then continue downward from there. The Opus hint was only partly usable; I corrected it in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_c87289bb/notes.md) because the official examples do not support splitting a centered stream to both sides on ties, and the implemented tie behavior is right-side exit.

The generator matches the family by sampling the same latent structure the task uses: sparse top blue columns, exactly one blank spacer row, one or two one-row horizontal red blockers, and short detours only when a blocker overlaps a stream. No new primitive was needed, so `arc2/dsl_ext.py` was left unchanged.
